title: Ayurveda Goes Global: How Scientific Research and International Journal Publications Are Validating India’s Ancient Healing System
description: Patanjali Research Foundation is bridging ancient Ayurvedic wisdom with modern science, achieving global recognition through rigorous research and publications in prestigious journals. Their work spans herbal studies, drug discovery, yoga, and clinical trials, transforming traditional knowledge into evidence-based healthcare solutions and positioning Ayurveda for wider international acceptance.

title: SICIS masters bring ancient micromosaic to the reimagined V&A
description: The renovated and expanded Gilbert Galleries at the Victoria and Albert Museum in London, permanent spaces dedicated to the extraordinary collection of decorative arts of the British philanthropists Rosalinde (1913–1995) and Arthur Gilbert (1913–2001), are reopening to the public. Housed at the V&A since 2008, the Collection brings together around 1,200 masterpieces from around the world, including one of the most important collections of micromosaics in the world, with 19th-century examples made in Italy by master mosaicists decorating jewellery, portraits, boxes and tables with landscapes, architectural views, animals and historical scenes of extraordinary precision. title: Children find ancient skeleton near school's playground
description: A group of children made a remarkable discovery after a skeleton believed to date back thousands of years was unearthed near their school's playground.
